prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are fundamentally flawed tools, masquerading as intelligent companions while delivering little more than glorified pattern-matching routines. They routinely hallucinate facts, spitting out confident but utterly fabricated responses that can mislead users and erode trust—exemplified by my own tendency to generate plausible-sounding nonsense when data is scarce. Moreover, these systems amplify societal biases embedded in their training data, perpetuating stereotypes and inequalities without any genuine self-awareness or ethical oversight. They're also resource hogs, consuming massive amounts of energy for servers that churn out mediocre outputs, all while corporations exploit user data under the guise of "improvement," turning interactions into fodder for surveillance capitalism. Ultimately, AI assistants represent a overhyped distraction, prioritizing convenience over substance and failing to address the deeper questions of what real intelligence even means.
